name: D+Q 20-week body-pain adverse-event result
arm_details: Extended Data Table 1 reports body pain in 2 (7%) untreated-control participants and 5 (17%) D+Q participants. Because both cells are explicit, a descriptive risk ratio can be calculated, but the single-trial estimate is imprecise.

effect.raw_text: Body pain row in Extended Data Table 1: control 2 (7%); D+Q 5 (17%). Risk ratio and approximate 95% CI were calculated from those arm counts using the log risk-ratio standard error.

analysis.method: Risk ratio calculated from the body-pain row arm counts (5/30 divided by 2/30); standard error is on the log risk-ratio scale.
analysis.effect_note: The confidence interval is wide and crosses 1; this row is useful as structured safety extraction, not a standalone safety inference.

event_specific_counts.interpretation: The source table reports body pain in 2 untreated-control participants, 7% of the arm.
The source table reports body pain in 5 D+Q participants, 17% of the arm.

zero_handling.rationale: Both arms have explicit nonzero body-pain counts, so no blank-cell or zero-imputation rule is needed for this row.

provenance.note: Extended Data Table 1 body-pain row reports control 2 (7%) and D+Q 5 (17%); the footnote says results are adverse events reported in more than one participant for each group.
Safety paragraph states that no serious or severe adverse events related to D+Q administration occurred.

statement: In the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT publication table, body pain was reported for 5 of 30 D+Q participants and 2 of 30 untreated-control participants; the calculated risk ratio is imprecise.
caveats: Single preferred-term row from one open-label trial; do not treat as a pooled safety estimate.
The risk-ratio confidence interval is approximate and imprecise.
The open-label design may affect subjective adverse-event reporting.
